descriptions.gorgias_search: Find the right Gorgias endpoint by natural-language query. Returns a ranked list of endpoint_ids (e.g. `tickets.list`, `customers.merge`) with their HTTP method, path, and one-line summary. ALWAYS call this before `gorgias_execute` — the endpoint_id you need is rarely guessable from English. Use specific phrasing (`list cancellation tickets`, not `find stuff about returns`). Returns at most 10 ranked matches; if your query is ambiguous, call again with a narrower phrase.
descriptions.gorgias_execute: Call any Gorgias endpoint by `endpoint_id` with a flat `params` map. Required: `endpoint_id` from `gorgias_search`. `params` is a map of strings for both path placeholders (e.g. `{"id": "123456789"}`) and query/body fields. For write endpoints, also pass `body` as a JSON-encoded string. Common foot-guns: (1) `--cursor` / `--limit` / `--order-by` go in `params`, not `body`. (2) `customers list` rejects `language` or `timezone` together with `cursor`/`limit`/`order_by` — pick one approach. (3) `events list` requires both `object_type` AND `object_id`. (4) `/search` indexes customers/agents/tags, NOT tickets — use `search` (local FTS) for ticket text search.
descriptions.context: Return the full machine-readable descriptor of this CLI: auth config, env vars, novel features, resource list, MCP transport, tool inventory. Call once at the start of a long session to load the descriptor into your working memory. Avoid re-calling within the same session — the descriptor doesn't change at runtime. Prefer over re-fetching tool descriptions because `context` summarizes WHEN to reach for each surface (live API vs local mirror vs compound workflow).
descriptions.sync: Pull Gorgias data into the local SQLite mirror so subsequent `search`, `sql`, `analytics`, `orphans`, `stale`, and `load` calls run against a warm offline cache. Pass `resources` as a comma-separated list (e.g. `tickets,customers,tags,macros`) or omit to sync everything syncable. `since 7d` is the typical first-run window; `since 0` is a full backfill. Run before any compound analysis that needs >100 records (Gorgias's per-page cap). Ticket `--since` uses documented `order_by=updated_datetime:desc` plus a local cutoff because `updated_datetime__gte` is not accepted by the live API. Caveat: `sync` is incremental by default but Gorgias's `messages` endpoint doesn't support temporal filters, so `messages` always re-pulls. Use `--max-pages 5` to bound a first run.
descriptions.search: Full-text search the local SQLite mirror (or hit Gorgias's POST /search when `--data-source live`). Defaults to the FTS5 mirror because Gorgias's /search indexes customers/agents/tags only — NOT tickets or messages. For ticket text search, run `sync` first. Common foot-gun: `--data-source live --type customer` reaches the API; `--type ticket` falls back to local because the live endpoint has no ticket scope.
descriptions.sql: Run a read-only SQL query against the local SQLite mirror. Single generic table: `resources` (columns: resource_type, id, data, synced_at). Filter by `resource_type` ('tickets', 'customers', 'tags', etc.) to scope a query. Body fields are JSON-encoded in the `data` TEXT column; access via `json_extract(data, '$.field')`. Use for one-off aggregations the compound workflows don't cover. Always paginate with `LIMIT` in the query — the mirror can carry tens of thousands of rows. Read-only: write queries are rejected.
descriptions.tail: Stream live ticket changes by polling Gorgias on a fixed interval. Emits one NDJSON event per new/updated ticket. Use when an agent needs to react to incoming traffic (triage, escalation). NOT a replacement for webhooks — Gorgias's poll latency is bounded by the interval, default 30s. Pass `--ticket-id` to scope to a single thread. Stops on Ctrl-C; in MCP, run with a bounded duration.
descriptions.export: Stream the local mirror to JSONL (or JSON array). Per-resource. Use to feed Gorgias data into BigQuery, dbt, or a one-off analysis pipeline. NOT for inter-system migration (use the Gorgias UI for that). Pairs with `sync` for a snapshot pipeline.
descriptions.import: Push JSONL records into Gorgias via API create/upsert calls. ONE record per line; each line must have the shape the target endpoint expects. Reserved for migrating data INTO Gorgias from a separate system — NOT for round-tripping the local mirror back to Gorgias. Skips records that already exist (idempotent on `external_id`).
descriptions.analytics: Aggregate the local mirror — counts, distributions, top-N tags, etc. Returns predefined slices, not arbitrary SQL (use `sql` for that). Designed for daily-digest agent prompts: 'how many open tickets', 'top intents this week', 'CSAT score distribution'. Always paired with a recent `sync`.
descriptions.orphans: List items missing a key field that production workflows expect — e.g. tickets with no `assignee_user`, customers with no `email`. Quick health check before a triage workflow. Pure local-mirror query; safe to run anytime. Returns IDs and excerpts, not full records.
descriptions.stale: List tickets with no activity for N days (default 7). Use to surface forgotten threads for an oncall review. Pairs well with `orphans` for the standard support-lead morning sweep. Reads `last_message_datetime` if present; otherwise `updated_datetime`.
descriptions.load: Show workload distribution per agent/assignee. Returns counts of open tickets per user, sorted descending. Use for capacity-balancing decisions or to find an idle agent before reassigning. Pure local-mirror query.
descriptions.workflow_archive: One-shot sync of ALL syncable resources (tickets, customers, macros, tags, etc.) to the local mirror. Use as a 'warm cold start' before doing anything analytics-heavy. Multi-minute operation. Subsequent reads can run `--data-source local` without a fresh sync. Use `--full` to ignore prior cursors and rebuild from scratch.
descriptions.workflow_status: Show local-mirror state: which resources are synced, row counts, last-sync timestamps. Use after `workflow_archive` to confirm everything landed. Pure local read.
